title: NOC 12202 – Insurance Underwriters | GTR Immigration
description: Insurance Underwriters play a critical role in Canada’s insurance sector by assessing risk, determining policy terms, and approving coverage for individuals and businesses. Classified under NOC 12202, this occupation continues to experience strong nationwide demand and offers promising immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.major_group: 122 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations
classification.minor_group: 1220 – Accounting, insurance, and related business administrative occupations

common_job_titles: Group underwriter Insurance underwriter Liability underwriter Property underwriter Casualty insurance products analyst-designer Insurance rating analyst
duties: Review individual and group applications for automobile, fire, health, life, marine, aircraft, and liability insurance
Evaluate new and renewal applications to assess risks, premiums, and coverage limits
Adjust pricing or coverage to enable acceptance of viable applications
Approve insurance policies and ensure regulatory compliance
Recommend individual or group insurance plan designs
Provide underwriting guidance to agents, consultants, and internal staff
Prepare underwriting reports and update insurance documentation
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school plus general insurance experience, or post-secondary education
Several years of underwriting-related on-the-job training
Completion of professional insurance industry courses and certification programs
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 13,200 new positions for Insurance Underwriters, compared with 11,900 job seekers—indicating favorable opportunities for experienced professionals seeking immigration to Canada.
additional_information: Strong hiring in property, casualty, health, and life insurance linesGrowing demand following regulatory expansion and complex risk portfoliosEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $41,500 to $103,700 CAD
Why Choose Canada in This Occupation?
Universal public healthcareSecure employment in a regulated industryCompetitive compensationStrong workplace protectionsWork–life balanceMulticultural communitiesClear routes from work permits to PR
